Throughput speedup over RocksDB on uniform scan-heavy workloads with 16 threads.

vldb2023-65.limiting_design_assumption: Random I/O on storage is prohibitively expensive, so system design must optimize for write performance by only making sequential writes, even if this choice degrades read performance via mechanisms like compaction and multi-file lookups.
vldb2023-65.state_or_ir: In-memory record cache, in-memory B+ tree index, and on-disk data segments composed of physically contiguous pages.
vldb2023-65.output: A persistent key-value store with high throughput for read-heavy and skewed write-heavy workloads.
vldb2023-65.equation: LSM (Sequential-Write Optimized) + Ha + Cc + Lo -> TreeLine
vldb2023-65.principle_composition_rationale: The composition enables a shift from a write-optimized LSM design to a read-optimized update-in-place design on modern hardware. Ha provides the fundamental premise: fast random writes on NVMe SSDs make update-in-place viable. However, update-in-place has its own drawbacks. Cc (via record caching) mitigates the write amplification for common skewed workloads, making writes competitive. Lo (via page grouping) mitigates the poor scan performance of naive update-in-place designs by restoring physical data locality.
vldb2023-65.one_sentence_mechanism: TreeLine re-enables update-in-place key-value stores for modern NVMe SSDs by using record-level caching for skewed workloads, physically grouping pages for fast scans, and forecasting inserts to reduce reorganization costs.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces the LSM sequential-write-only assumption with an update-in-place design, leveraging fast random writes on NVMe SSDs. It uses record-level caching to mitigate write amplification for skewed workloads, which are common but inefficient for traditional page-caching B+ trees.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increased metadata overhead in the cache compared to page caching. The design is not optimized for uniform update-heavy workloads.

key_deltas.summary: Improves scan performance and reduces insert costs in the update-in-place design by grouping logically adjacent pages into physically contiguous on-disk segments and using workload-aware forecasting to pre-allocate space for future inserts.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Added complexity of the reorganization process. Forecasting accuracy depends on workload stability. The linear models introduce an error tolerance (`epsilon`) that requires leaving space on pages, potentially increasing space amplification.

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The entire design premise of switching from LSMs to an update-in-place model.
Ha.why_it_applies: The paper's central argument is that NVMe SSDs, with sufficient parallelism, have random write performance comparable to sequential writes, which invalidates the core assumption of LSMs and makes update-in-place designs viable again.

Cc.name: Common-Case Specialization
Cc.mechanism_trigger: The use of a record cache instead of a page cache.
Cc.why_it_applies: The system is specialized for skewed workloads, which are identified as a common case. Record caching efficiently handles hot, scattered records by keeping them in memory, reducing I/O amplification for updates, which is a major drawback of naive update-in-place designs.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The choice of record caching over page caching.
Wa.why_it_applies: The design explicitly considers that in many real applications, the working set (hot records) is uncorrelated with the key sort order, making page caching ineffective. The record cache adapts to this workload property by caching individual hot items regardless of their on-disk page.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The insert forecasting mechanism.
Wa.why_it_applies: The system tracks the distribution of inserts over time using a histogram and uses this forecast to proactively leave empty space in pages during reorganization. This adapts the physical data layout to the expected insert workload to reduce future I/O.

Lo.name: Locality of Reference
Lo.mechanism_trigger: The page grouping mechanism.
Lo.why_it_applies: Page grouping physically co-locates pages that store adjacent key ranges into contiguous on-disk segments. This explicitly creates physical locality to improve the performance of range scans by enabling long, sequential reads.

La.name: Learned Approximation
La.mechanism_trigger: Using piece-wise linear models to find pages within a multi-page segment.
La.why_it_applies: A simple linear model is fit to the key distribution within a segment to approximate the mapping from a key to its page. This replaces a traditional, potentially larger, index structure for pages within a segment, reducing the overall in-memory index size.

vldb2023-66.paper_title: PIM-tree: A Skew-resistant Index for Processing-in-Memory
vldb2023-66.goal: To design an ordered index for Processing-in-Memory (PIM) systems that achieves both low communication cost and high load balance, regardless of the degree of skew in data and queries.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Throughput improvement over a prior skew-resistant PIM solution (PIM-balanced skip list).
Throughput improvement over a range-partitioned PIM solution.
Communication cost compared to state-of-the-art non-PIM indexes.

vldb2023-66.limiting_design_assumption: A static partitioning of the key space into subranges, with each subrange assigned to a PIM module, will result in balanced workloads across PIMs. This assumption fails under data or query skew.
vldb2023-66.primary_tool_specific_type: Hybrid CPU/PIM data structure with adaptive scheduling
vldb2023-66.state_or_ir: The PIM-tree data structure, a hybrid B+-tree and chunked skip list, partitioned across a host CPU and distributed PIM modules.
vldb2023-66.output: Results of batch-parallel index operations (Get, Predecessor, Insert, Delete, Scan).
vldb2023-66.equation: Static Range Partitioning + Fp + Wa + Lo -> PIM-tree
vldb2023-66.principle_composition_rationale: The baseline's static partitioning fails under skew. The composition of Fp and Wa creates a dynamic, adaptive system that routes work to the appropriate resource (CPU for contended work, PIM for parallel work), solving the load balance problem. Lo then optimizes the data layout itself through replication and chunking, reducing communication for the common 'push' case and making the entire system more efficient.
vldb2023-66.one_sentence_mechanism: PIM-tree is a skew-resistant PIM index that dynamically decides whether to process queries on PIM nodes or pull contended index nodes to the CPU, combined with selective replication and chunking to reduce communication.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces 'push-pull search' to dynamically decide whether to execute a query on a PIM module (push) or fetch the index node to the CPU for processing (pull), based on workload contention.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increased CPU-side complexity to count queries, track contention, and coordinate the push/pull decisions across rounds.

key_deltas.summary: Reduces communication and improves locality using 'shadow subtrees' (selective replication) and 'chunking' (increasing node fanout).
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increased space complexity from shadow subtrees and increased implementation complexity for updates (Insert/Delete), which must maintain these replicated structures.

Fp.name: Function Placement
Fp.mechanism_trigger: The 'push-pull search' mechanism.
Fp.why_it_applies: The system explicitly and dynamically decides where to place the 'search' computation: either on the distributed PIM nodes ('push') or on the central host CPU ('pull'), based on runtime conditions.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The contention-counting part of the push-pull decision.
Wa.why_it_applies: The decision to push or pull is driven directly by a measured property of the current workload batch: the number of queries targeting a specific index node, which is a direct measure of skew.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The multi-round nature of the push-pull search.
Ad.why_it_applies: The system adapts its execution strategy at runtime based on observed contention. Wa is more specific about the trigger (workload) and Fp is more specific about the action (placement), making Ad a good supporting principle for the overall adaptive behavior.

Lo.name: Locality of Reference
Lo.mechanism_trigger: The creation and use of shadow subtrees.
Lo.why_it_applies: Shadow subtrees are an explicit replication mechanism to create local shortcuts for remote data accesses, thereby improving locality and reducing communication. The paper states this is for 'caching remote accesses at PIM modules to build local shortcuts'.

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The chunking of skip list nodes.
Ha.why_it_applies: Chunking is explicitly motivated by the hardware characteristics of the PIM system, specifically to increase access granularity to achieve better local memory bandwidth on the PIM processors.

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: Using a shadow subtree to traverse multiple levels of the index locally.
Rc.why_it_applies: The shadow subtree is a materialized, replicated artifact. Using it avoids the work of repeated remote pointer chasing. Lo is more primary as it explains the placement/replication intent, while Rc describes the benefit of having that replicated data available.

vldb2023-67.paper_title: Dotori: A Key-Value SSD Based KV Store
vldb2023-67.goal: To design a key-value store for Key-Value SSDs (KVSSDs) that provides critical features like transactions and snapshots while achieving high performance by leveraging the unique properties of the KV interface.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Dotori's throughput improvement over other stores in uniform write-heavy YCSB workloads with large values.
Dotori's throughput improvement over the next best store in the read-heavy YCSB B workload.
Dotori's total write amplification compared to RocksDB in the YCSB A medium workload.
Dotori's P99.99 read latency compared to ScyllaDB in the YCSB A run.

vldb2023-67.baseline_name: Block-device based KV stores and their naive adaptations to KVSSDs
vldb2023-67.limiting_design_assumption: The storage interface is block-based, which forces the host system to manage data placement and garbage collection (compaction) via complex indexes (e.g., B+trees, LSM-trees). This leads to read-modify-write cycles for index updates and high write amplification.
vldb2023-67.state_or_ir: The OAK-tree, represented in-memory as a B+tree and on-disk as a collection of sparse, per-node logs stored as KV pairs.
vldb2023-67.output: A persistent mapping from user keys to versioned internal keys stored on the KVSSD.
vldb2023-67.equation: Block-based Indexing + Ha (Hardware-Aware) + Wv (Work Avoidance) + Op (Optimistic Design) -> Dotori
vldb2023-67.principle_composition_rationale: The design is fundamentally Hardware-Aware (Ha) of the KVSSD interface. This awareness enables a new persistence strategy that provides Work Avoidance (Wv) by eliminating the read from the read-modify-write cycle for index updates. To manage the consequences of these 'blind' writes (i.e., not knowing node fullness), the system uses Optimistic Design (Op) to speculatively track node size and defer the expensive work of a full node reconstruction until a split is likely.
vldb2023-67.one_sentence_mechanism: Dotori introduces a hardware-aware B+tree for Key-Value SSDs that avoids read-modify-write cycles for index updates by appending changes as sparse, per-node logs, and optimistically reconstructs nodes via parallel reads only when a split is likely.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces the read-modify-write pattern for index node updates with an append-only, out-of-order logging scheme where node modifications are written as new, small key-value pairs directly to the KVSSD without first reading the original node.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Reading a cold index node requires fetching multiple log fragments from the KVSSD and merging them, which can increase read latency, especially if the number of logs per node (`MAX_LOGS`) is high.

key_deltas.summary: Manages node splits optimistically and reconstructs cold nodes via parallel reads. Since nodes are not read on write, an in-memory counter speculates on node fullness. When a split threshold is crossed, all of a node's log fragments are read from the KVSSD in parallel to build the full node in memory and perform the split if necessary.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: A high `MAX_LOGS` value can increase read latency by overwhelming the I/O queue. The speculative split check can trigger a 'false positive', causing an unnecessary full node read if the speculation was incorrect (e.g., many updates were to existing keys, not new inserts).

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The OAK-tree's append-only logging scheme, which writes index updates as distinct KV pairs (`N:log_id`) to the KVSSD.
Ha.why_it_applies: The entire design is predicated on the KVSSD's native key-value interface, which allows sparse, non-contiguous data to be written and retrieved efficiently by key. The paper states this design is 'only practical when the underlying storage device is a KVSSD'.

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The reconstruction of an index node by issuing parallel, asynchronous `retrieve` commands for all its constituent log fragments.
Ha.why_it_applies: This parallel reconstruction is only possible because the KVSSD allows efficient, concurrent retrieval of multiple, non-contiguous items by key. A block device would require sequential scanning or complex metadata to achieve the same result.

Wv.name: Work Avoidance: skip work that cannot alter externally observable results
Wv.mechanism_trigger: When updating an index node, the OAK-tree writes a new log entry without first reading the existing node or its logs from storage.
Wv.why_it_applies: The read operation performed by baseline copy-on-write B+trees is avoided. This read is guaranteed not to alter the final logical state of the index, as the new update is simply appended to the node's history. The paper explicitly contrasts this with baselines where 'the target leaf needs to be read and re-written in its entirety'.

Al.name: Abstraction Lifting
Al.mechanism_trigger: The system design operates on key-value pairs for index persistence, not blocks and offsets.
Al.why_it_applies: The system leverages the higher-level KV interface to free the host from managing physical data layout. This allows the OAK-tree to 'sparsely write index data without needing to worry about where the data lies on the disk'.

Op.name: Optimistic Design
Op.mechanism_trigger: Using an in-memory counter to track the number of entries in an index node without reading the node from storage. The expensive operation (reading all logs) is deferred until the counter crosses a split threshold.
Op.why_it_applies: The system optimistically assumes that most modifications will not trigger a split and proceeds with a cheap in-memory counter increment. It only pays the cost of verification (reading the node) when a split becomes likely. The paper states, 'Dotori simply initially assumes that every modification of an index node is an insert.'

vldb2023-68.paper_title: DINOMO: An Elastic, Scalable, High-Performance Key-Value Store for Disaggregated Persistent Memory
vldb2023-68.problem_domain: Key-value store for disaggregated persistent memory
vldb2023-68.goal: To build a key-value store for disaggregated persistent memory (DPM) that simultaneously achieves high common-case performance, scalability, and lightweight online reconfiguration.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Minimum throughput improvement over Clover at 16 KNs across all evaluated workloads.
Time for Dinomo to recover from a KN failure, including merging pending logs and redistributing ownership.
Time for a shared-nothing baseline (Dinomo-N) to reorganize data after a KN failure, during which throughput is zero.

vldb2023-68.limiting_design_assumption: A shared-everything architecture, where all KNs share ownership of data and can access/modify all data, is necessary to achieve lightweight reconfiguration. This assumption forces the system to incur significant consistency overheads (cache coherence, contention) and suffer from poor cache locality.
vldb2023-68.state_or_ir: Key-to-KN ownership mapping (hash ring), KN-local caches containing values and shortcuts.
vldb2023-68.output: A key-value store that is performant, scalable, and elastic.
vldb2023-68.equation: Shared-Everything DPM KVS + Fp + Ad + Cz -> Dinomo
vldb2023-68.principle_composition_rationale: The principles combine to resolve the tension between performance and elasticity. Fp and Cz provide high performance and scalability by partitioning ownership to avoid coordination, creating high-locality caches similar to a shared-nothing system. Ad then provides an adaptive, cost-based caching policy to make the best use of this local cache memory to minimize network access, further boosting performance.
vldb2023-68.one_sentence_mechanism: Dinomo achieves performance and elasticity in a disaggregated key-value store by partitioning logical data ownership to avoid cache consistency overheads, while using an adaptive caching policy to minimize network access.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces Ownership Partitioning (OP), where logical ownership of data is partitioned across KNs while the data itself remains physically shared on DPM. This avoids cache consistency overheads of the shared-everything baseline while enabling lightweight reconfiguration, unlike shared-nothing systems.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Standard ownership partitioning can create hotspots under skewed workloads, which requires a separate mechanism (selective replication) that re-introduces some consistency overheads for hot keys.

key_deltas.summary: Implements Disaggregated Adaptive Caching (DAC), a policy that dynamically balances caching full values versus pointers ('shortcuts') to data on DPM to minimize network round trips based on workload and available memory.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The policy has some complexity and requires tracking access frequencies and performing calculations on cache hits/misses to make promotion/demotion decisions.

Fp.name: Function Placement
Fp.mechanism_trigger: Assigning exclusive ownership of a logical data partition to a single KVS Node (KN).
Fp.why_it_applies: This mechanism places the 'ownership' function—the right to cache and modify data without coordination—onto a specific node to create high cache locality.

Cz.name: Coordination Avoidance
Cz.mechanism_trigger: The exclusive ownership of data partitions by KNs.
Cz.why_it_applies: By ensuring only one KN owns (and thus caches) a piece of data at any time, the system completely avoids the need for cache coherence protocols, a major coordination bottleneck in the shared-everything baseline.

Ec.name: Elasticity
Ec.mechanism_trigger: Reconfiguring the cluster by re-assigning ownership metadata without moving the underlying data on DPM.
Ec.why_it_applies: This mechanism is what makes reconfiguration 'lightweight' and enables the system to elastically add or remove KNs in response to load changes, a key design goal.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The Disaggregated Adaptive Caching (DAC) policy dynamically changes the ratio of full values to shortcuts (pointers) in the KN-local cache.
Ad.why_it_applies: The policy explicitly and automatically adapts to 'changes in workload patterns and to the changes in the aggregate memory space for caching at KNs' to optimize performance.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: The promotion decision in DAC, which uses a formula to compare the benefit (saved network round trips) of caching a full value against the cost (incurred round trips from evicting shortcuts).
Cm.why_it_applies: This is a direct implementation of a cost model to make a planning decision about cache contents, aiming to minimize the cost metric of network round trips.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: DAC uses access frequency (for shortcut eviction) and recency (for value demotion) to manage the cache.
Wa.why_it_applies: The policy is aware of workload characteristics like temporal and frequency locality to make more effective caching decisions.

vldb2023-69.paper_title: Sim-Piece: Highly Accurate Piecewise Linear Approximation through Similar Segment Merging
vldb2023-69.goal: To improve the compression ratio of Piecewise Linear Approximation (PLA) for time-series data by exploiting similarities among the generated line segments, especially for strict error thresholds where traditional PLA is less competitive than lossless compression.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Average improvement in compression ratio over competing PLA algorithms.
Factor by which Sim-Piece is faster than the second and third best approaches in terms of space efficiency, respectively.
Sim-Piece achieves an equivalent compression ratio to the second-best approach while using an error threshold (𝜖) that is, on average, less than 37% of the threshold used by that competitor.

vldb2023-69.baseline_name: Traditional Piecewise Linear Approximation (PLA) algorithms (e.g., Swing, Slide, Mixed)
vldb2023-69.limiting_design_assumption: Baseline PLA algorithms represent each generated line segment independently. They do not exploit redundancy or similarity between different segments to achieve a more compact representation.
vldb2023-69.state_or_ir: A list of slope intervals, where each interval is represented by `⟨a_l, a_u, t_s⟩` (lower slope, upper slope, start timestamp) and is associated with a quantized starting value `b`.
vldb2023-69.output: A list of groups, where each group contains a quantized starting value `b`, representative upper and lower slope bounds, and a list of starting timestamps for all merged segments.
vldb2023-69.equation: Independent Segment PLA + Se + Rc -> Sim-Piece
vldb2023-69.principle_composition_rationale: The principles form a two-stage process. First, Semantically Explicit (Se) representation transforms each segment from a single line into an interval of valid lines, exposing the inherent flexibility in the approximation. This creates the opportunity for optimization. Then, Reuse of Computation (Rc) and Equivalence-based (Ep) partitioning exploit this explicit representation by finding an optimal grouping of segments that can share a single representative line, thus reusing the representation and minimizing storage.
vldb2023-69.one_sentence_mechanism: Sim-Piece improves PLA compression by quantizing segment start-points, representing each segment as an interval of valid slopes, and then optimally merging overlapping intervals to represent many segments with a single shared slope.

key_deltas.summary: Represents each line segment not with a single slope, but as an interval of valid slopes anchored at a quantized starting y-value, making the approximation's flexibility explicit.
key_deltas.primary_metric_improved: Enables downstream compression; does not directly improve a metric itself.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Introduces a quantization step that slightly alters the segment's starting point, but this is kept within the overall error bound `𝜖`.

key_deltas.summary: Optimally merges segments that share a quantized starting value and have overlapping slope intervals, reducing the total number of unique segment descriptions that must be stored.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Adds a sorting and merging phase (Phase 2), but this overhead is shown to be small, and the overall algorithm is often faster than competitors.

Se.name: Semantically Explicit
Se.mechanism_trigger: Representing each segment as an interval of valid slopes `[a_l, a_u]` instead of a single chosen slope.
Se.why_it_applies: This mechanism makes the inherent flexibility of the approximation explicit. The interval `[a_l, a_u]` is a semantic declaration of the entire solution space for a segment's slope, which is then consumed by the merging algorithm.

Cr.name: Consistency Relaxation
Cr.mechanism_trigger: The quantization of a segment's starting value `v_s` to the nearest multiple of `𝜖`.
Cr.why_it_applies: This is a deliberate, bounded relaxation of the segment's starting point. It trades exactness for the opportunity to create common starting points (`b` values) that enable subsequent grouping of segments. The relaxation is controlled by the error bound `𝜖`.

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: Merging multiple segments with overlapping slope intervals into a single group that is represented by one shared slope.
Rc.why_it_applies: The core of the compression gain comes from reusing a single representation (the group's slope) for many different segments, avoiding the cost of storing each one individually. This is a form of representational reuse.

Ep.name: Equivalence-based
Ep.mechanism_trigger: The algorithm for partitioning the set of slope intervals into the minimum number of groups.
Ep.why_it_applies: The problem is explicitly modeled as partitioning a set of items (intervals) into the minimum number of equivalence classes (groups of intersecting intervals, or cliques in the interval graph). The greedy algorithm optimally solves this partitioning problem.

Bo.name: Bottleneck-Oriented
Bo.mechanism_trigger: The entire merging mechanism is designed to reduce the storage footprint of the PLA representation.
Bo.why_it_applies: The paper's motivation is that the storage size of traditional PLA representations is a bottleneck that makes them uncompetitive with lossless compression for small error thresholds. The merging mechanism is designed specifically to alleviate this storage bottleneck.

vldb2023-70.paper_title: The FastLanes Compression Layout: Decoding > 100 Billion Integers per Second with Scalar Code
vldb2023-70.goal: To accelerate the decoding of common Light-Weight Compression (LWC) schemes by redesigning the compression layout to improve data-parallelism for SIMD and scalar execution, while remaining portable and future-proof across heterogeneous hardware.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Decoding throughput achieved by FastLanes.
Peak bit-unpacking speed on an Intel AVX512 CPU for 1024-tuple vectors.
End-to-end query performance improvement versus uncompressed in-memory scans on an 8-thread benchmark.
End-to-end query performance improvement versus scalar decoding on an 8-thread benchmark.

vldb2023-70.baseline_name: Existing LWC schemes with SIMD-width-specific layouts
vldb2023-70.limiting_design_assumption: The physical data layout for compressed data must be optimized for a specific, existing SIMD register width, and values must be processed in their logical sequential order.
vldb2023-70.state_or_ir: A sequence of 1024 integer values, representing a vector in a columnar database.
vldb2023-70.output: A reordered and interleaved bit-packed representation of the integer vector, optimized for parallel decoding across heterogeneous hardware.
vldb2023-70.equation: SIMD-width-specific layouts + Gr + Ha + Cz -> FastLanes
vldb2023-70.principle_composition_rationale: The design combines a generalized abstraction of future hardware (Gr) with a concrete, hardware-aware data layout (Ha) that permutes data to eliminate micro-architectural coordination between SIMD lanes (Cz). Generalization provides portability and future-proofing, while the hardware-aware layout unlocks maximum parallelism on any target.
vldb2023-70.one_sentence_mechanism: FastLanes accelerates columnar data decoding by using a generalized, virtual 1024-bit SIMD layout and a novel 'Unified Transposed Layout' that permutes tuples to break data dependencies, enabling massive data-parallelism on any hardware.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces a virtual 1024-bit SIMD register and a corresponding interleaved bit-packing layout to maximize data-parallelism and ensure future-proofness across heterogeneous and evolving hardware ISAs.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The layout chunk size is fixed at 1024 values, which increases the minimum access granularity and can slightly worsen compression ratios compared to smaller, adaptive chunk sizes.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces the 'Unified Transposed Layout,' which reorders tuples within a 1024-value vector to break sequential data dependencies inherent in decoding schemes like DELTA and RLE, enabling fully data-parallel SIMD execution.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Tuple order is permuted within each 1024-value vector, which may require restoration for order-sensitive operations. It also increases the number of base values needed per vector, adding a small compression overhead (e.g., 3% for a 64-bit type).

Gr.name: Generalized Design
Gr.mechanism_trigger: The design of a single data layout and virtual instruction set based on a 1024-bit register.
Gr.why_it_applies: The system preemptively targets a virtual, wider-than-available hardware abstraction (1024-bit register) to create a single, unified design that works efficiently across all existing and anticipated SIMD widths and ISAs, avoiding hardware-specific implementations.

Gr.name: Generalized Design
Gr.mechanism_trigger: The specific '04261537' tile ordering.
Gr.why_it_applies: The layout is not just transposed for one data type; it is a *unified* layout. A single, specific reordering was invented to work correctly and efficiently for all possible lane widths (8, 16, 32, and 64 bits), generalizing the dependency-breaking solution across all data types.

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The use of an interleaved bit-packed layout.
Ha.why_it_applies: The layout distributes bits of logically subsequent values across different SIMD lanes (round-robin). This is explicitly done to align with the parallel nature of SIMD units and avoid expensive cross-lane instructions like PERMUTE or BITSHUFFLE that would be needed with a naive sequential layout.

Ha.name: Hardware-Aware
Ha.mechanism_trigger: The 'Unified Transposed Layout' reorders tuples.
Ha.why_it_applies: The tuple reordering is explicitly designed to create independent work for SIMD lanes. It breaks data dependencies that are 'SIMD-unfriendly' by ensuring that each lane operates on a self-contained sequence, perfectly matching the hardware's parallel execution model.

Si.name: Simplicity
Si.mechanism_trigger: The definition of a minimal virtual instruction set and reliance on scalar code with auto-vectorization.
Si.why_it_applies: The system avoids the complexity and technical debt of maintaining platform-specific SIMD intrinsics by defining a simple set of operators and implementing them in portable scalar code that compilers can automatically optimize, simplifying development and ensuring future-proofness.

Cz.name: Coordination Avoidance
Cz.mechanism_trigger: The reordering of tuples to create independent DELTA sequences for each SIMD lane.
Cz.why_it_applies: The mechanism transforms the DELTA decoding problem from a single, long dependency chain into many short, independent chains. This eliminates the need for coordination (data transfer or sequential processing) between adjacent SIMD lanes, which is a form of coordination avoidance at the micro-architectural level.

vldb2023-71.paper_title: Toward Quantity-of-Interest Preserving Lossy Compression for Scientific Data
vldb2023-71.goal: To provide error control on downstream analysis results, known as Quantities of Interest (QoIs), during lossy compression, eliminating the need for trial-and-error tuning while maximizing the compression ratio.

concrete_numbers.meaning: The compression ratio improvement over state-of-the-art compressors under the same QoI tolerance.
The peak compression ratio improvement observed on the NYX dataset compared to the best existing compressor for a QoI error tolerance of 1E-3.

vldb2023-71.baseline_name: State-of-the-art error-controlled lossy compressors (e.g., SZ, ZFP, MGARD)
vldb2023-71.limiting_design_assumption: The baseline assumption is that controlling error on raw data with a uniform, global error bound is a sufficient proxy for controlling error in downstream analyses. This forces users into a manual, time-consuming 'trial and error' process to find a suitable bound for their specific QoI.
vldb2023-71.state_or_ir: The mechanism uses the user-specified QoI function, its error tolerance, and the original data values to derive per-point error bounds.
vldb2023-71.output: A stream of per-datapoint error bounds that are fed into the quantization stage of a prediction-based lossy compressor.
vldb2023-71.equation: QoI-Agnostic Compression + Se + Cp + Ep -> QoI-Preserving Compression
vldb2023-71.principle_composition_rationale: This composition transforms a semantically-blind compression system into one that is guided by user intent. Se provides the system with a high-level, meaningful goal (the QoI). Cp provides the mathematical machinery to translate that goal into low-level operational parameters (per-point error bounds). For complex, multi-variable goals, Ep provides a crucial reduction strategy, rewriting the problem into a sequence of simpler problems that Cp can solve.
vldb2023-71.one_sentence_mechanism: The system preserves user-specified Quantities of Interest (QoIs) in lossy compression by analytically mapping the QoI error tolerance to per-datapoint error bounds, which guide a prediction-based compressor's quantization stage.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces the baseline's uniform, global error bound on raw data with per-point error bounds derived by analytically mapping the user's high-level QoI error tolerance down to the raw data domain.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Compression speed is reduced due to the extra computation for error bound estimation, quantization, and encoding. The derived error bounds themselves must also be compressed, adding some storage overhead.

key_deltas.summary: Enables preservation of complex multivariate QoIs by reducing the problem to a sequence of simpler univariate QoI preservation problems, leveraging the sequential processing nature of prediction-based compressors.
key_deltas.primary_metric_improved: Extends the scope of preservable QoIs to include multivariate functions like regional averages.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The mechanism is only applicable to prediction-based compression pipelines that process data sequentially and make decompressed values immediately available. It is incompatible with block-based transform compressors.

Se.name: Semantically Explicit
Se.mechanism_trigger: The user provides a QoI function (e.g., f(x)=x^2) and a tolerance τ, which explicitly defines the semantics of the desired quality.
Se.why_it_applies: The core idea is to replace the semantically poor baseline constraint (e.g., L-infinity norm on raw data) with a semantically rich, user-defined QoI. The system directly uses this explicit semantic information to derive tailored, per-point error bounds, closing the gap between raw data error and analysis outcomes.

Cp.name: Constraint-based
Cp.mechanism_trigger: The system formulates the 'error bound derivation problem' Q(f, τ, x) which seeks an error bound ε such that the QoI error constraint |f(x) - f(x')| ≤ τ is satisfied.
Cp.why_it_applies: The paper's theoretical foundation is explicitly framed as a constraint satisfaction problem. For each data point, it solves for the largest possible raw data error bound (ε) that satisfies the user's constraint on the QoI error (τ).

Mo.name: Modularity
Mo.mechanism_trigger: The implementation of the QoI-preserving framework as a 'QoI module' that is decoupled from the main compression pipeline.
Mo.why_it_applies: The paper explicitly highlights the modular design, which decouples the QoI derivation logic from the compressor. This allows the framework to be adapted to new QoIs and different prediction-based compression algorithms without changing the core components.

Au.name: Automation
Au.mechanism_trigger: The system automatically derives the correct error bounds, replacing the manual 'trial and error' process required with baseline compressors.
Au.why_it_applies: A key benefit highlighted by the paper is the automation of what was previously a manual, time-consuming tuning process for scientists. The constraint-based mechanism automates the selection of compression parameters.

Ep.name: Equivalence-based
Ep.mechanism_trigger: Theorem 4, which proves that preserving a multivariate QoI can be reduced to preserving a sequence of univariate QoIs.
Ep.why_it_applies: The mechanism transforms a complex, multi-variable constraint problem into an equivalent sequence of simpler, single-variable problems. This rewriting is the key theoretical move that makes preserving multivariate QoIs tractable within their framework.

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: When processing data point x_i, the mechanism uses the already computed decompressed values x'_j for all j < i.
Rc.why_it_applies: The reduction of the multivariate problem relies on reusing the results of previous compression steps (the decompressed values) as known constants to simplify the constraint for the current data point. This is a direct reuse of a previously computed artifact.

vldb2023-72.goal: To reduce the cost of running a user-defined Video Extract-Transform-Load (V-ETL) job on a fixed budget of hardware resources, while providing throughput guarantees to keep up with the video stream.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Cost reduction achieved by Skyscraper over a static baseline on the MOT workload at comparable quality.
Cost reduction achieved by Skyscraper over an adapted Chameleon* baseline on the MOT workload at better quality.
Time for Skyscraper to make a tuning decision on a single CPU core.

vldb2023-72.baseline_name: Content-adaptive systems without throughput guarantees (e.g., Chameleon) and static configuration systems.
vldb2023-72.limiting_design_assumption: The core assumption is that video processing must either be statically configured for peak load, or can adapt to content without long-term planning for resource constraints (like buffer space and cloud budgets) and throughput guarantees. Prior systems are "agnostic to lag and the hardware resources they run on" [p.2].
vldb2023-72.state_or_ir: Content categories (KMeans cluster centers), forecasted category frequencies, and a knob plan (a set of histograms assigning knob configurations to content categories).
vldb2023-72.output: A sequence of (knob configuration, task placement) decisions for incoming video segments.
vldb2023-72.equation: Resource-Unaware Adaptation + Ad (Adaptive Processing) + Cm (Cost-based) + Ec (Elasticity) -> Skyscraper
vldb2023-72.principle_composition_rationale: The principles form a two-timescale control loop. Adaptive Processing (Ad) is split into a slow, predictive planner and a fast, reactive executor. The planner uses Cost-based (Cm) optimization on a workload forecast to set a global resource policy. The executor implements this policy reactively, using Elasticity (Ec) via cloud bursting as a safety valve to meet throughput guarantees when local resources are overwhelmed.
vldb2023-72.one_sentence_mechanism: Skyscraper reduces video analytics cost by using a predictive, cost-based planner to create a long-term resource allocation strategy and a lightweight reactive switcher to execute the plan using buffering and elastic cloud bursting to meet throughput guarantees.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces a predictive, long-term planning component that forecasts future workload characteristics (as a distribution of 'content categories') and uses a linear program to create an optimal plan for rationing compute resources (knob configurations) over a long time horizon (e.g., days).
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Requires historical data for training. Adds complexity of an offline phase and periodic re-planning. Performance is sensitive to forecast accuracy, especially over long horizons.

key_deltas.summary: Implements a lightweight, reactive execution component ('knob switcher') that enforces the long-term plan while guaranteeing throughput. It classifies current content, selects a knob configuration from the plan, and chooses the cheapest task placement (on-premise vs. cloud bursting) that is guaranteed not to overflow the local buffer.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The reactive classification is heuristic and can be inaccurate. The need to avoid buffer overflows can cause deviations from the optimal plan, potentially reducing quality.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The periodic execution of the knob planner, which uses a forecast of future workload to create a new knob plan.
Ad.why_it_applies: The system's core strategy is to adapt its processing configuration (knobs) based on the characteristics of the video content. This delta implements the predictive, long-horizon part of that adaptation.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The knob switcher's frequent decisions to select a knob configuration based on the current content category.
Ad.why_it_applies: This is the reactive, short-timescale component of the system's adaptation. It adapts to the content as it arrives, guided by the long-term plan from Δ1.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: The formulation and solving of a linear program to generate the knob plan.
Cm.why_it_applies: The planner explicitly optimizes an objective (maximize quality) subject to a resource constraint (compute budget). The LP formulation directly models the cost and quality of different knob choices for different content types.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The use of a forecasting model to predict the future distribution of content categories.
Wa.why_it_applies: The system's planning is explicitly driven by knowledge of the workload's statistical properties over time. The forecast makes the system aware of future workload patterns, enabling proactive resource rationing.

Ec.name: Elasticity
Ec.mechanism_trigger: The decision to offload UDF execution to on-demand cloud workers (AWS Lambda) when the on-premise cluster and buffer are insufficient to meet throughput.
Ec.why_it_applies: The system dynamically acquires and releases cloud resources ('cloud bursting') to handle peak load, which is a canonical example of elasticity.

Fp.name: Function Placement
Fp.mechanism_trigger: The knob switcher selecting a 'task placement' that specifies which UDFs run on-premise and which run in the cloud.
Fp.why_it_applies: This is the direct mechanism used to implement elasticity. The system explicitly decides where to place computation (on-premise vs. cloud) to manage cost and throughput.

vldb2023-73.paper_title: EQUI-VOCAL: Synthesizing Queries for Compositional Video Events from Limited User Interactions
vldb2023-73.goal: To automatically synthesize complex, declarative queries over video data from a small number of user-provided positive and negative examples, without requiring database expertise or declarative specifications.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Reduction in query synthesis time compared to baseline systems.
Speedup in executing each candidate query compared to the Quivr baseline for a sample query (TQ3).
Number of queries explored by EQUI-VOCAL versus the Quivr baseline for a sample query (TQ3), demonstrating a much smaller search.

vldb2023-73.limiting_design_assumption: An effective query can be found by exhaustively enumerating and evaluating all possible queries that are consistent with a small set of user-provided examples.
vldb2023-73.state_or_ir: A DSL for spatio-temporal scene graph queries, translated to SQL.
vldb2023-73.output: A ranked list of top-k declarative queries that find a user-specified event in videos.
vldb2023-73.equation: Enumerative Query-by-Example + Bo + Cm + Ad -> EQUI-VOCAL
vldb2023-73.principle_composition_rationale: The composition addresses the intractability of query synthesis from examples. Bo and Cm work together to replace exhaustive search with a tractable, cost-guided heuristic search, making synthesis fast. Ad then integrates user feedback into this search loop, allowing the system to adaptively steer towards the user's true intent with minimal labeling effort, correcting for the heuristic nature of the search.
vldb2023-73.one_sentence_mechanism: Synthesizes complex video queries from user examples by replacing exhaustive enumeration with a cost-guided beam search that adaptively requests new labels via active learning to prune the search space.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces exhaustive, enumerative query synthesis with a heuristic, execution-guided beam search to prune the vast search space of possible queries, dramatically reducing synthesis time.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The search is no longer guaranteed to be optimal; it might miss the best query if it is pruned early. The quality of the result depends on the beam width and the scoring function.

key_deltas.summary: Integrates active learning into the synthesis loop to iteratively request labels for the most informative video segments, reducing user labeling effort and improving robustness to noise.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Requires an interactive user in the loop, which introduces human latency. The system's progress is tied to the user's availability and labeling accuracy.

Bo.name: Bottleneck-Oriented
Bo.mechanism_trigger: The use of beam search to limit the number of explored query candidates at each step of synthesis.
Bo.why_it_applies: The primary performance bottleneck is the combinatorial explosion of the query search space. The entire beam search strategy is designed to manage this bottleneck by focusing computation on a small set of promising candidates.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: Evaluating candidate queries against the set of labeled examples and using their F1 score to decide which queries to retain for the next expansion step.
Cm.why_it_applies: The F1 score acts as a cost model to rank and select among alternative query plans (candidates). The system prunes branches of the search space that have a high 'cost' (low F1 score).

Gd.name: Goal-Directed
Gd.mechanism_trigger: The overall query-by-example framing, where the system must synthesize a query that satisfies the user-provided examples.
Gd.why_it_applies: The system synthesizes a complex artifact (a query) from a high-level specification of the goal (the positive and negative examples). Bo and Cm are more specific about the *how* of the search, while Gd describes the overall problem structure.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The active learning loop that selects new video segments for the user to label based on the current set of candidate queries.
Ad.why_it_applies: The query synthesis process adapts its search direction at runtime based on new information (user labels). The choice of which examples to request is determined by the system's current state of uncertainty.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The use of disagreement-based sampling to select which video segments to present to the user for labeling.
Wa.why_it_applies: The 'workload' is the user's unknown query intent. The system becomes aware of this workload by actively probing the user for labels on the most uncertain or contentious examples, where candidate queries disagree, thereby maximizing information gain about the user's intent.

vldb2023-74.paper_title: Optimizing Video Analytics with Declarative Model Relationships
vldb2023-74.goal: To design a declarative interface for users to specify ML model relationships (Relational Hints) and a query engine (VIVA) that uses these hints to automatically optimize complex video analytics queries, navigating the performance-accuracy tradeoff.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Maximum performance improvement from using hints while meeting user accuracy requirements.
The performance variation across different manual query plans for a news analysis query, highlighting the large optimization space.
Average percentage of plans eliminated by VIVA's pruning heuristics.

vldb2023-74.baseline_name: Manual query optimization or systems treating ML UDFs as black boxes
vldb2023-74.limiting_design_assumption: A query optimizer has no semantic knowledge about the relationships between different ML models (implemented as UDFs) and therefore cannot automatically explore optimizations like model replacement or filtering. The optimizer treats UDFs as black boxes, and users must manually rewrite queries to try different optimizations.
vldb2023-74.output: An optimized query execution plan that meets a user-specified accuracy requirement.
vldb2023-74.equation: Black-Box UDF Optimization + Se + Ep + Cm -> VIVA
vldb2023-74.principle_composition_rationale: This composition transforms traditional query optimization by first making model relationships Semantically Explicit (Se). This exposed knowledge enables an Equivalence-based (Ep) planner to generate a rich space of alternative, approximately-equivalent query plans. A Cost-based (Cm) optimizer then navigates this space to find the most efficient plan that satisfies user constraints.
vldb2023-74.one_sentence_mechanism: VIVA introduces a declarative hint interface for users to specify semantic relationships (e.g., replacement, filtering) between ML models, enabling a cost-based query optimizer to explore a large space of plans to find the fastest one that meets a user's accuracy constraint.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces 'Relational Hints,' a declarative SQL-like interface for users to specify domain knowledge about how ML models can replace or filter for each other, making these relationships known to the query optimizer.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The system's effectiveness depends on users providing accurate and useful domain knowledge through hints.

key_deltas.summary: Implements a hint-aware, cost-based query optimizer that systematically generates, validates, and costs a large space of alternative query plans, selecting the best one that meets a user's accuracy target.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Query optimization latency increases due to the larger search space. Accuracy estimation relies on a user-provided 'canary input' which must be representative of the larger dataset.

Se.name: Semantically Explicit
Se.mechanism_trigger: The `CREATE HINT` DDL for `CAN REPLACE` and `CAN FILTER`.
Se.why_it_applies: This mechanism's sole purpose is to make the semantic relationships between otherwise opaque ML models explicit to the query optimizer, allowing it to reason about plan transformations.

Ah.name: Advisory Hinting
Ah.mechanism_trigger: The system's treatment of hints as suggestions that are only applied if they meet the user's accuracy requirement.
Ah.why_it_applies: The paper explicitly states that hints are not enforced but are options for the optimizer to consider, which is the definition of an advisory hint. The optimizer can choose to ignore a hint if the resulting plan violates the accuracy constraint.

Ex.name: Extensibility
Ex.mechanism_trigger: The ability to add new models and optimizations by simply registering new hints.
Ex.why_it_applies: The hint interface provides a clean way to extend the optimizer's capabilities with new models and relationships without modifying the core planning and optimization logic.

Ep.name: Equivalence-based
Ep.mechanism_trigger: The planner's use of `CAN REPLACE` and `CAN FILTER` hints to generate alternative query plans.
Ep.why_it_applies: The hints define approximate semantic equivalences between different query sub-plans (e.g., running model M vs. model H). The planner's core function is to apply these equivalence rules to transform the original query tree into a set of candidate trees.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: The optimizer estimating the latency and monetary cost of each generated plan to select the best one.
Cm.why_it_applies: VIVA explicitly builds a cost model based on profiled model latencies and hardware prices, estimates selectivity, and uses these factors to calculate the total cost of each plan, which is the basis for its final selection.

Cp.name: Constraint-based
Cp.mechanism_trigger: The optimizer selecting a plan that meets the user's specified accuracy requirement.
Cp.why_it_applies: The user's accuracy target is a hard constraint on the optimization problem. The optimizer's goal is not just to find the lowest-cost plan, but the lowest-cost plan that *satisfies* the accuracy constraint.

vldb2023-75.paper_title: Motiflets - Simple and Accurate Detection of Motifs in Time Series
vldb2023-75.goal: To define a more intuitive and robust method for time series motif discovery by replacing the hard-to-tune distance threshold parameter 'r' with a more intuitive motif set size 'k', and to automate the selection of input parameters.

concrete_numbers.meaning: The proposed approximate algorithm is a 2-approximation of the exponential-time exact algorithm.
On real-world datasets, the ratio of the extent found by the approximate algorithm to the exact one is consistently over 91% for varying k, and 89% for varying n.
On semi-synthetic datasets with ground truth, k-Motiflets achieved 100% precision on most datasets, while the best competitors had a median precision around 75%.
The approximate k-Motiflets algorithm is faster than all competitors, though the difference is less than a factor of 2 for most.

vldb2023-75.limiting_design_assumption: All prior motif set discovery methods require the user to specify a distance threshold 'r' as a core input parameter. This parameter is a real value that is difficult to intuit, and small changes to it can lead to grossly different results, requiring extensive trial-and-error.
vldb2023-75.state_or_ir: A pairwise z-normalized distance matrix between all subsequences.
vldb2023-75.output: The set of k subsequences with the minimal extent (the top k-Motiflet) and its extent value.
vldb2023-75.equation: r-parameterized Motif Discovery + Gd + Si + Au -> k-Motiflets
vldb2023-75.principle_composition_rationale: The composition inverts the user interaction model for a search problem. Goal-Directed (Gd) and Simplicity (Si) work together to reframe the problem from specifying a low-level, unintuitive mechanism parameter ('r') to a high-level, simple goal ('k'). Automation (Au) then leverages this new, stable 'k'-based formulation to create a feedback loop that analyzes system behavior across 'k' to automatically tune parameters, a task that was infeasible with the brittle 'r'-based approach.
vldb2023-75.one_sentence_mechanism: Replaces the traditional, hard-to-tune distance-threshold parameter 'r' for time series motif discovery with an intuitive cardinality parameter 'k', and automates parameter selection by finding structural 'elbow points' in the function mapping 'k' to motif similarity.

key_deltas.summary: Inverts the problem parameterization from being based on a distance threshold 'r' to being based on a desired motif set size 'k'. Instead of finding the largest set within distance 'r', it finds the set of size 'k' with the minimum possible distance.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The method has a 'blind spot for same k-retrieval': if two distinct motif sets of the same size 'k' exist, the algorithm will only report the one with the smaller extent.

key_deltas.summary: Automates the selection of the two key input parameters, motif length 'l' and set size 'k', by analyzing the structural properties of an 'Extent Function' (EF) curve.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The elbow detection method is heuristic, relying on a threshold parameter, and assumes the data contains meaningful motifs. It may produce less meaningful results on random data.

Gd.name: Goal-Directed
Gd.mechanism_trigger: The redefinition of the motif discovery problem from 'find the largest set within radius r' to 'find the set of size k with minimum radius'.
Gd.why_it_applies: The user specifies a high-level goal (the desired cardinality 'k') rather than a low-level mechanism parameter (the distance 'r'). The system is responsible for synthesizing the plan to achieve this goal while optimizing a cost (the extent). The paper explicitly states this 'turns the MD problem upside-down'.

Si.name: Simplicity
Si.mechanism_trigger: The choice of an integer cardinality 'k' as the primary user-facing parameter instead of a real-valued distance 'r'.
Si.why_it_applies: The paper's central argument is that 'k' is 'considerably more intuitive and easier to set' than 'r'. This design choice simplifies the user interaction and removes a major source of complexity and brittleness from the system.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: The algorithm's search for the k-Motiflet with the minimum 'extent'.
Cm.why_it_applies: Given the goal of finding a set of size 'k', the system uses the 'extent' (max pairwise distance) as a cost model to compare and select among all possible sets of that size. The objective is to find the set that minimizes this cost.

Au.name: Automation
Au.mechanism_trigger: The algorithms for learning meaningful values for 'k' and 'l' from the data without user intervention.
Au.why_it_applies: This mechanism directly automates the previously manual and tedious task of parameter tuning. The paper states it presents extensions 'that can automatically learn the right values for the two input parameters'.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The use of the Extent Function (EF) curve, which is derived from the input time series, to determine the optimal parameters.
Wa.why_it_applies: The system adapts its parameters ('k' and 'l') based on the specific characteristics of the input data ('workload'). The shape of the EF curve, which dictates the chosen parameters, is entirely dependent on the structure of the time series being analyzed.

Ho.name: Human Observability
Ho.mechanism_trigger: The introduction and use of 'elbow plots' based on the Extent Function.
Ho.why_it_applies: The paper presents the EF curve as a visual tool for 'guided extraction of meaningful motif set sizes'. This plot makes the internal state and trade-offs of the system (similarity vs. cardinality) observable to a human, aiding in interpretation and parameter selection.

vldb2023-76.paper_title: Fast and Scalable Mining of Time Series Motifs with Probabilistic Guarantees
vldb2023-76.goal: To develop a fast and scalable method to find the top-k motifs in very large time series with probabilistic guarantees on the correctness of the result.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Attimo's performance compared to the GPU-based state of the art, Scamp.
The size of the largest time series Attimo can process on a single CPU-based machine.
Runtime of Attimo vs. estimated runtime of Scamp-gpu on the 1-billion-point Seismic dataset to find the top motif.

vldb2023-76.limiting_design_assumption: Finding the top-k motifs requires computing or approximating all pairwise subsequence distances, making the algorithm inherently Ω(n^2) in complexity.
vldb2023-76.state_or_ir: LSH hash values for each subsequence, stored in lexicographically sorted arrays for each repetition. A priority queue stores the current top-k candidate motifs.
vldb2023-76.output: The top-k motifs (pairs of subsequences) with a probabilistic guarantee of correctness (1-δ).
vldb2023-76.equation: All-pairs distance computation + Pd + Ad -> Attimo
vldb2023-76.principle_composition_rationale: The baseline's exhaustive computation is intractable. Probabilistic Design (Pd) provides the core mechanism (LSH) to prune the search space by trading exactness for speed. However, LSH is notoriously hard to tune. Adaptive Processing (Ad) makes Pd practical by creating a control loop that automatically tunes LSH's selectivity based on the observed data distribution, ensuring a user-defined quality guarantee is met efficiently.
vldb2023-76.one_sentence_mechanism: Attimo uses a self-tuning, data-adaptive Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) scheme to probabilistically prune the vast majority of distance computations for finding time series motifs, by iteratively relaxing hash selectivity until a user-defined error guarantee is met.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces the baseline's exhaustive all-pairs distance computation with a probabilistic pruning approach using Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH), which generates a much smaller set of candidate pairs by hashing similar subsequences to the same buckets.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The result is no longer exact but is correct with a user-controlled probability (1-δ). This introduces LSH-specific parameters (K, L, r) that need to be managed.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces a self-tuning, data-adaptive control loop that automatically selects LSH parameters to meet a user-specified failure probability, making the system both easier to use and more efficient on datasets with prominent motifs.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Execution time becomes data-dependent; the algorithm is faster on 'easier' datasets where motifs stand out, but potentially slower on 'harder' ones, in contrast to the baseline's predictable runtime.

Pd.name: Probabilistic Design
Pd.mechanism_trigger: The use of Locality Sensitive Hashing (LSH) to generate candidate pairs.
Pd.why_it_applies: LSH is a probabilistic data structure where the collision of two items is a probabilistic function of their distance. The entire algorithm is built around this property to provide probabilistic guarantees on the result.

Bo.name: Bottleneck-Oriented
Bo.mechanism_trigger: The design choice to use LSH to avoid the O(n^2) pairwise distance computations.
Bo.why_it_applies: The paper explicitly identifies the O(w*n^2) complexity of the naive all-pairs distance computation as the performance bottleneck. The LSH mechanism is specifically designed to attack and mitigate this bottleneck.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The iterative process of decreasing the LSH prefix length based on the currently found top-k motifs until a stopping condition is met.
Ad.why_it_applies: The algorithm explicitly adapts its parameters (the effective hash length) at runtime based on the data distribution as reflected by the distances of candidate motifs found so far. The paper highlights this as a key feature.

Au.name: Automation
Au.mechanism_trigger: The self-tuning nature of the algorithm that automatically finds a suitable configuration of LSH parameters.
Au.why_it_applies: The mechanism automates the difficult and data-dependent task of setting LSH parameters, which was a barrier to prior LSH-based approaches and would otherwise require manual effort.

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: The mechanisms for avoiding duplicate distance computations for pairs that collide repeatedly, both within a single repetition (across different prefix lengths) and across multiple repetitions.
Rc.why_it_applies: The system explicitly tracks and avoids re-computing the distance for a pair that has already been evaluated, thus reusing the result of the first computation (or the fact that it was computed).

vldb2023-77.paper_title: OneShotSTL: One-Shot Seasonal-Trend Decomposition For Online Time Series Anomaly Detection And Forecasting
vldb2023-77.problem_domain: Time series analysis (decomposition, anomaly detection, forecasting)
vldb2023-77.goal: To develop an online seasonal-trend decomposition (STD) algorithm with O(1) update complexity, enabling low-latency, high-throughput processing of time series data for anomaly detection and forecasting.

concrete_numbers.meaning: Online update time complexity, compared to the baseline's O(T).
Speedup compared to batch STD methods.
Average latency to process a single data point.
Time series data points processed per second on a single CPU core.

vldb2023-77.baseline_name: Existing online STD methods (e.g., OnlineSTL, OnlineRobustSTL)
vldb2023-77.limiting_design_assumption: Online updates for seasonal-trend decomposition require computation proportional to the length of the seasonal period (T), resulting in O(T) complexity per data point.
vldb2023-77.primary_tool_specific_type: Online algorithm for solving a linear system
vldb2023-77.state_or_ir: A fixed-size state for the online Doolittle factorization (L_o, D_o, b_o) and a seasonal buffer (v).
vldb2023-77.output: Decomposed trend (τ_t), seasonal (s_t), and residual (r_t) components for each new data point y_t.
vldb2023-77.equation: O(T) Online STD + Rc + Ad -> OneShotSTL
vldb2023-77.principle_composition_rationale: The composition achieves both high performance and robustness. Rc provides the core speedup to O(1) by incrementally maintaining the solution to the decomposition problem, while Ad adds robustness by allowing the model to adapt to shifts in the underlying data patterns, which would otherwise violate the model's assumptions and degrade accuracy.
vldb2023-77.one_sentence_mechanism: OneShotSTL achieves O(1) online time series decomposition by reformulating the problem as a linear system with a banded matrix and incrementally maintaining its Doolittle factorization, reusing the previous step's computation.

key_deltas.summary: Achieves O(1) online update complexity by reformulating the STD problem as a linear system with a banded matrix and solving it incrementally using an online Doolittle factorization, avoiding a full re-solve at each time step.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: The online formulation is an approximation of the full batch JointSTL problem, though experiments show it provides 'sufficiently good decomposition'.

key_deltas.summary: Handles seasonality shifts by monitoring the residual signal for anomalies and, upon detection, searching a local window of possible time shifts to find the one that best explains the data, improving robustness.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increases computational complexity by a constant factor O(I * H) when an anomaly is detected, but this is infrequent in practice (e.g., <1%).

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: The arrival of a new data point at time t+1.
Rc.why_it_applies: The OnlineDoolittle algorithm explicitly carries forward and reuses the factorization state (L_o, D_o, b_o) from the previous time step (t) to compute the factorization for the current time step. This avoids recomputing the factorization for the entire history of data points.

Wv.name: Work Avoidance: skip work that cannot alter externally observable results
Wv.mechanism_trigger: The factorization of the matrix A_t+1.
Wv.why_it_applies: The online factorization algorithm avoids re-processing the top-left sub-matrix of A_t+1 because it is identical to the top-left of A_t. This skipped work is guaranteed not to change the final result of the factorization, as it only depends on values that are already computed and stored from the previous step.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The detection of an anomaly in the residual signal r_t by the streaming NSigma algorithm.
Ad.why_it_applies: The system adapts its model of seasonality in response to a runtime signal. Upon detecting an anomaly, it changes its behavior by searching for a new seasonal phase offset Δt that minimizes the residual, effectively correcting the model online.

vldb2023-78.paper_title: Towards Designing and Learning Piecewise Space-Filling Curves
vldb2023-78.goal: To design a space-filling curve (SFC) that uses different mapping schemes for different data subspaces (a piecewise SFC) to optimize query performance by adapting to data and query workload properties.

concrete_numbers.meaning: I/O cost reduction of BMTree over Z-curve on the UNI dataset with various workloads.
I/O cost reduction of BMTree over Z-curve on the TIGER dataset with various workloads.
Computing the ScanRange (SR) proxy metric is 20x faster than executing queries under PostgreSQL for training.

vldb2023-78.limiting_design_assumption: A single, global mapping scheme (Bit Merging Pattern, or BMP) is sufficient to optimize query performance across the entire data space, regardless of local variations in data or query distribution.
vldb2023-78.state_or_ir: A partially or fully constructed Bit Merging Tree (BMTree).
vldb2023-78.output: A fully constructed BMTree that represents a piecewise SFC mapping function.
vldb2023-78.equation: Single-Scheme SFC + Wa + La + Cm -> BMTree
vldb2023-78.principle_composition_rationale: This composition transforms a static, global indexing scheme into a learned, adaptive one. Wa defines the new objective: create a piecewise mapping that is sensitive to the workload. La and Cm provide the mechanism to achieve this objective by learning how to partition the space and assign local mappings, using a cost model (Cm) to guide the learned search (La) through the vast design space, a task intractable for heuristics.
vldb2023-78.one_sentence_mechanism: Replaces a single global space-filling curve with a learned, piecewise curve that uses different mappings for different data subspaces, constructed by a Monte Carlo Tree Search agent to optimize a query performance proxy.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces piecewise SFCs, where different mapping schemes (Bit Merging Patterns, or BMPs) are used for different data subspaces, allowing the curve to adapt to local data and query distributions.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increased design complexity and the need for a construction algorithm that can explore the large space of possible piecewise SFCs.

key_deltas.summary: Uses a reinforcement learning (RL) agent based on Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to automatically construct an optimized BMTree, instead of relying on brittle heuristics.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Requires a training phase with representative data and query workloads, which adds computational cost and time. The quality of the resulting SFC depends on the quality of the training inputs.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: The BMTree partitions the data space and generates a specific Bit Merging Pattern (BMP) for each subspace.
Wa.why_it_applies: The entire motivation for piecewise SFCs is to adapt the mapping function to local data and query workload properties. The structure of the BMTree is explicitly optimized for a given workload.

Mo.name: Modularity
Mo.mechanism_trigger: The BMTree structure decomposes the single global mapping problem into a collection of independent mapping problems, one for each subspace defined by a root-to-leaf path.
Mo.why_it_applies: The system is structured as a composition of local mapping functions (BMPs), each responsible for a distinct subspace. This modularizes the overall mapping.

La.name: Learned Approximation
La.mechanism_trigger: Using Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) to learn a policy for constructing the BMTree.
La.why_it_applies: The system uses a learned model (the MCTS policy) to approximate the solution to a very complex optimization problem: finding the best BMTree structure. This replaces brittle, hand-coded heuristics with a learned decision-making process.

Cm.name: Cost-based
Cm.mechanism_trigger: The MCTS search and the Greedy Action Selection algorithm are guided by a reward signal derived from the `ScanRange` metric.
Cm.why_it_applies: The `ScanRange` metric is explicitly designed as a computationally cheap proxy for the true query performance cost (I/O, latency). The learning process directly optimizes this cost model to make decisions.

vldb2023-79.paper_title: Adaptive Indexing of Objects with Spatial Extent
vldb2023-79.problem_domain: In-memory adaptive indexing for multidimensional spatial data
vldb2023-79.goal: To enable quick exploration of large multidimensional spatial data in main memory by building an index incrementally in response to queries, without the overhead of a priori indexing.

concrete_numbers.meaning: The AIR-tree outperforms previous multidimensional adaptive indexing methods in per-query and cumulative response time by at least one order of magnitude.
On the ROADS dataset with a clustered workload, AIR-tree achieves an up to 205x speedup in total response time compared to other adaptive methods.
The AIR-tree consistently outperforms a pre-built static R-tree in cumulative time over at least the first thousand queries.

vldb2023-79.baseline_name: Single-dimension-per-level adaptive indexing (e.g., QUASII, Cracking KD-trees)
vldb2023-79.limiting_design_assumption: Existing adaptive indexing methods dedicate each level of a multilevel index to a single dimension. This is inefficient for non-point multidimensional objects with spatial extent, requiring workarounds like query transformation and post-processing.
vldb2023-79.state_or_ir: An in-memory static array holding spatial objects and a dynamically growing R-tree index structure pointing into ranges of this array.
vldb2023-79.output: Query results and a refined, more complete R-tree index over the data.
vldb2023-79.equation: Single-dimension-per-level indexing + Wa + Ad + Bo -> AIR-tree
vldb2023-79.principle_composition_rationale: These principles form a feedback loop for efficient, incremental index construction. Wa provides the signal (queries) that dictates where to build the index. Ad provides the core mechanism for acting on that signal at runtime (cracking and tree expansion). Bo makes this adaptive process practical and scalable by targeting and mitigating the primary performance bottleneck (the cost of precise boundary updates during cracking).
vldb2023-79.one_sentence_mechanism: AIR-tree builds an in-memory R-tree for spatial objects incrementally, using range query boundaries to partition data while applying R-tree quality heuristics to guide the splits and lazy maintenance to accelerate the process.

key_deltas.summary: Replaces single-dimension-per-level adaptive indexing with a multi-dimensional R-tree structure that is built and refined based on the query workload, natively handling objects with spatial extent.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Increased complexity of cracking logic compared to simple dimensional splits, as it must manage MBBs, overlap, and margins.

key_deltas.summary: Introduces lazy boundary maintenance during cracking to make the adaptive indexing process efficient, especially for higher dimensions.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Newly created leaf nodes may have temporarily 'loose' MBB boundaries, which could lead to redundant tree traversals in subsequent queries until those nodes are further refined or finalized.

Wa.name: Workload-Aware
Wa.mechanism_trigger: An incoming range query that intersects an unrefined ('irregular') region of the data.
Wa.why_it_applies: The index structure is not pre-built but is assembled and shaped directly by the queries processed by the system. The paper's central premise is that the index 'assembles itself in response to queries'.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The 'cracking' operation, which partitions a leaf node based on a query's boundaries and expands the index tree.
Ad.why_it_applies: The system dynamically reorganizes its internal data structure (the R-tree index) at runtime in response to incoming queries, progressively refining it from a single root node to a full index.

Pd.name: Probabilistic Design
Pd.mechanism_trigger: The stochastic extension (s-AIR) performs an additional crack on a randomly chosen pivot after the query-driven cracks.
Pd.why_it_applies: This mechanism uses randomness (sampling to find a 'mediocre' element) to improve robustness against skewed workloads, which can cause poor performance in standard cracking.

Bo.name: Bottleneck-Oriented
Bo.mechanism_trigger: The decision to update MBB boundaries only along the cracking dimension, rather than all dimensions, during a data partition.
Bo.why_it_applies: This mechanism directly targets a known performance bottleneck. The paper explicitly states that eager boundary maintenance on all dimensions caused performance to deteriorate when moving to 3D, and lazy maintenance was introduced to solve this specific problem.

vldb2023-80.paper_title: Adaptive Indexing in High-Dimensional Metric Spaces
vldb2023-80.problem_domain: Similarity search in high-dimensional metric spaces
vldb2023-80.goal: To develop an adaptive index for high-dimensional metric spaces that avoids high upfront construction costs, making it suitable for environments with short-lived data and a relatively small number of queries.

concrete_numbers.meaning: The cumulative cost of AV-tree converges to that of the pre-built state-of-the-art MVP-tree after a few thousand queries.
AV-tree becomes much faster than a linear scan after only a few queries.
Index size for the MNIST dataset after 1000 queries, which is lightweight compared to the data size.

vldb2023-80.limiting_design_assumption: A complete, high-quality index must be constructed prior to processing any queries. This 'build-then-query' model is inefficient if the index construction cost is not amortized over a large number of queries.
vldb2023-80.state_or_ir: A binary search tree (the AV-tree) where nodes represent past queries (vantage points), and a single data array that is physically reordered (cracked) into partitions corresponding to the tree's leaf nodes.
vldb2023-80.output: A set of object identifiers that satisfy a range or k-NN similarity query.
vldb2023-80.equation: Static Indexing + Ad + Rc + Ig -> AV-tree
vldb2023-80.principle_composition_rationale: This composition transforms a static, pre-built index into a dynamic, on-demand structure. Ad provides the high-level strategy of building the index incrementally. Rc provides the mechanism for this by capturing the work of query processing into a persistent index structure. Ig provides the geometric foundation for the index partitions, ensuring that the pruning is both correct and effective in general metric spaces, which is where prior adaptive methods failed.
vldb2023-80.one_sentence_mechanism: AV-tree builds an index for high-dimensional metric spaces adaptively by reusing query work to partition data based on distance to query centers, using the triangle inequality for correct pruning.

key_deltas.summary: Instead of building a full index upfront, the AV-tree is constructed incrementally by physically partitioning (cracking) the data array in response to each query, reusing the query processing work to build the index.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: Initial queries are slower than on a pre-built index because they perform scanning and partitioning work. The resulting index structure is dependent on the query workload.

key_deltas.summary: The system partitions data using distance-based hyperspheres around query centers (vantage points), rather than using axis-aligned hyperrectangles, which are ineffective in high dimensions.
key_deltas.tradeoffs_or_costs: This approach is only applicable to metric spaces where a distance function satisfying the triangle inequality is defined. It is not a general-purpose multidimensional index.

Ad.name: Adaptive Processing
Ad.mechanism_trigger: The arrival of a query that traverses to a leaf node in the AV-tree.
Ad.why_it_applies: The system's physical data layout and index structure are not static; they evolve at runtime in direct response to the queries being processed. The paper's central theme is 'adaptive indexing'.

Rc.name: Reuse of Computation
Rc.mechanism_trigger: The `crack-in-two` procedure, which is executed as part of answering a query.
Rc.why_it_applies: The distance computations and comparisons performed to find a query's results are not discarded. This work is materialized as a physical reordering of the data array and a new node in the AV-tree, which is then reused to accelerate subsequent queries.

Ig.name: Invariant-Guided
